title: Does anyone relate to Asuka more
display_title: Does anyone relate to Asuka more
wikitext:
[]: Does anyone relate to Asuka more?             Quote Post by zeldapsychology » Sat Apr 04, 2015 6:31 pm Most anime sites and character anime sites etc. touch upon depression and Shinji but I have Asperger's (from my understanding of the character). Asuka questioning who she is trying to be tough and then finally crying and breaking down going into a catatonic coma depression was really deep. I deal with depression myself and no I don't have parent issues like Shinji or certainly the mom hanging issues but the questioning who she is mask thing putting on a "social mask" of who she really is hit me hard. She comes out of her shell in EoE and kicks ass but I'm still questioning myself in the "social mask" aspect of my life. Can anyone relate to Asuka as I do???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 05, 2015 2:40 am I do. Having started school officially a year early, and then skipped a year in high school (ending up the youngest, geekiest member of class and thus not at all socially integrated), I ended up taking the normally 16+ exams at age 14, and went up to Cambridge with a scholarship just days after my 18th birthday. And at the end of my first year, I hit the first set of maths exams that I couldn't just breeze through based on classes and homework, and I only came in at about 14th place across the year. That was a massive shock to the system -- revision and maths exams had never gone together before -- and really hit my self-confidence during what ought to have been the happiest post-exam, early summer, part of the college year. Fast forwards nearly 30 years, and I'm watching NGE episode 16, and Asuka's plight hits me straight in that wound that I had thought long healed. I have no formal diagnoses of anything, but some years ago, I took part in a study about the genetic basis of mathematical ability, and as well as the obvious cheek swab, I was also run through the Baron-Cohen autism spectrum test. The grad student who was administering the tests looked at my results and asked when I had been diagnosed, having scored over 40 --I had to explain that that wasn't a thing back in the 1960s.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sun Apr 05, 2015 5:49 am Me but only with certain aspects of her. I believe that everyone can relate in a way with a character from an anime, movie, book, game etc. or with some of his/her aspects.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 05, 2015 7:58 am The only times I can relate to her are when I'm under so much stress that sublimating my pain into self-loathing no longer cuts it and I start violently and bitterly lashing out as well. But otherwise, mmmnope.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Fri Apr 10, 2015 10:49 am I can definitely relate to her when it comes to matters of the heart. Pretty harsh on myself when I fail. The problem is I don't relate with just any one character, I can relate with Shinji, Asuka, Rei, Misato, and Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Apr 10, 2015 1:52 pm Started rewatching the series and found that I actually relate with her a lot. The line 'I do it because I want to be able to praise myself' stands out a lot for me, and I really do react to tests and standardized assessment in the same way she does

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 10, 2015 2:15 pm I relate to Anno. By that I mean that, since all of the characters are facets of Anno, I tend to relate to all of them, Asuka included. I relate to Rei's schizoid tendencies, to Shinji's avoidance and depression, to Asuka's ire at being supremely prepared for a task that no longer matters, to being a lazy smart person who's contemptuous of the world around her, to being a dreamer who just doesn't care , to being a confused child who doesn't understand what's going on, to being a professional who drinks to excess to blow off steam from the pressures of work, to being absolutely sure I have the right of things even though I might actually be way off-base . . . I relate to all of them, man. Mostly the Children (as a unit, mind; I relate strongly to all of them in different ways), but the other characters too.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 10, 2015 7:45 pm The only thing I relate to Asuka is a tendency to cut down others before building up myself. And I don't much like this quality, so seeing it in her makes me really dislike her.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 10, 2015 8:07 pm NemZ wrote: The only thing I relate to Asuka is a tendency to cut down others before building up myself. And I don't much like this quality, so seeing it in her makes me really dislike her.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Apr 10, 2015 8:17 pm NemZ wrote: The only thing I relate to Asuka is a tendency to cut down others before building up myself. And I don't much like this quality, so seeing it in her makes me really dislike her.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Apr 10, 2015 9:23 pm Guy Nacks wrote: This really seems to echo Anno's own answer to the question of what he dislikes about Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Apr 12, 2015 11:40 am Come to think of it, I was watching Episode 22 today and realized Asuka's 'I hate everyone' bathtub speech is one I have unknowingly echoed in the past. Disturbing. My outward presentation of myself is very Rei-like but my inner consciousness gravitates towards an Asuka-like personality more often.

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Mon Apr 13, 2015 4:44 am NemZ wrote: The only thing I relate to Asuka is a tendency to cut down others before building up myself. And I don't much like this quality, so seeing it in her makes me really dislike her.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Mon Apr 13, 2015 10:31 am I find myself relating far more with Asuka than I do with the other characters in the show, though Shinji is a very close second. I've been both at different points in my life.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Mon Apr 13, 2015 10:01 pm I don't relate to Asuka but my Significant Other certainly does. There's a few character aspects they both share, including a horrific incident in their childhood that in turn sparked her very tsundere personality. I know that when my SO feels threatened, she will go through certain stages in the same way that Asuka does. This starts out as belittling others when they feel insecure, and escalates to outright verbal attacks on others if they feel very threatened. Typically what threatens them most is failure: either the fear of it, or the fear of being perceived by others as a failure (which is often worse in their mind). When really facing a crisis they then go and hide themselves behind closed doors until things cool off, often treating others as if they were dead or non-existent because dealing with them is just too much. The "fake" side of the personality is also there, pretending to be happy or pretending to be cute to gain attention. This is often just another stage and not sincere. Surely she has good points, no? Absolutely, and so does Asuka. There are moments in EVA where Asuka truly has her good moments and the same is also true with my SO. Often these good moments are with what others may say is child-like behavior, but to me is really who she is inside. And I enjoy those moments the most. I never really get enough of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Apr 13, 2015 11:13 pm Before he transcended morality Chuckman knew the pain of not losing close a relative but seeing their faculties greatly diminished and dealing with frightening, confusing, and hurtful changes in their behavior and personality at a critical age.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Apr 13, 2015 11:49 pm I identify with Asuka a lot, and it seems like the people who I've talked to about the show, all people I've met through punk rock, identify with her a lot too. She tries to "win at life" on her own terms, and her failure to do so, coupled with the emotional fallout, is one of the most resonant parts of the series. Asuka is also interesting in that she can be romantically forward, but is confused about what she really wants from another man. I feel like my interactions with women are, sadly, similar to Asuka's interactions with men. Episode 15 is very real to me.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Tue Apr 14, 2015 2:59 am Relating to Asuka is pretty tricky for me. Sure, her backstory is sad considering that her mother went crazy, strangled her and then committed suicide with little Asuka walking in on the scene. She has a fundamental need to prove to others and to herself that she's strong and doesn't need anyone else. But a bitch is still a bitch. She's a 14-15 year old girl, so I should cut her some slack. There's also how Nerv essentially fed Asuka her own hype since she was a child in addition to Misato's failings as a guardian in letting Asuka hold on to her delusional self-image until Arael happened. But there's a difference between being self-confident and competitive and being a pretentious, self-important bully and Asuka strongly leans towards the latter. Maybe if Asuka herself didn't make things so difficult or had more moments when she was actually approachable and human, she'd seem more identifiable. As someone with Asperger's myself, I don't really see the connection. I just always thought that Asuka was either a narcissistic or had some kind of inferiority/superiority complex.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 14, 2015 6:27 am LordThaeon wrote: She's a 14-15 year old girl, so I should cut her some slack. There's also how Nerv essentially fed Asuka her own hype since she was a child in addition to Misato's failings as a guardian in letting Asuka hold on to her delusional self-image until Arael happened.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Tue Apr 14, 2015 6:37 am Bagheera wrote: She was 13 throughout the series. On the contrary, the fact she has so few of those moments is why she's so identifiable. Most people aren't a perfect balance of good and bad, and those with shitty life experiences don't tend to be all sunshine and roses. She's a histrionic type, actually. Narcissists seek power, which is very much not her thing.

Asuka questioning who she is trying to be tough and then finally crying and breaking down going into a catatonic coma depression was really deep. I deal with depression myself and
